hook.text: Transfer money: debit one account, credit another. Atomicity means both happen or neither happens.

buildup.text: Atomic means indivisible. The transaction is one unit of work. No observer sees it half-done.

discovery.text: If the server crashes mid-transaction, the write-ahead log replays and undoes uncommitted changes on recovery.

twist.text: Atomicity doesn't mean instant. The transaction takes time, but the effects appear atomically at commit.

climax.text: Without atomicity, every failure requires manual cleanup. With it, the database handles recovery for you.

punchline.text: Atomicity: all or nothing. The database cleans up failures.

quiz.question: How does a database achieve atomicity after a crash?
options: It replays the write-ahead log and undoes any uncommitted transactions
It restores from the most recent backup
The operating system prevents crashes from affecting the database
